mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate below 0.25 percent forces institutional investors to rotate from low-yielding fixed income into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 60-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent signals substantial economic slack that ensures continued monetary accommodation, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts generates a joint demand impulse that exceeds the predictive power of either series in isolation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS changes and HOUST changes produces a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During zero-lower-bound monetary regimes, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal strengthening supply-side activity that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to exhibit a rank correlation above 0.05 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low realized consumer price inflation, as proxied by muted month-over-month changes in CPIAUCSL, eliminates the threat of near-term monetary tightening and sustains elevated equity valuations, producing positive 5-day forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 700 thousand units signal a depressed residential construction sector that drags on aggregate demand and construction-related corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 700.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate signals a robust labor market recovery that boosts consumer income expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 97 indicates weak manufacturing and utility output that suppresses corporate revenue growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when INDPRO is below 97.
